_README: Edit the site copy here, then run: node _build/build.js  (from the managers-matrix-site folder). Light inline HTML is allowed: <strong> for bold, <em> for the gold highlight, <a href> for links. Standalone quote fields (book.quote, bookTeaser.quote, book.dedication) are plain text; the quotation marks are added automatically. Use {episodes} as a placeholder for the live episode count.

hero.sub: Short, focused leadership pieces for managers in motion. Built around the challenges modern leaders face every day, narrated with warm, cinematic storytelling and zero buzzwords.

bookTeaser.heading: THE STORY NO ONE TELLS YOU BEFORE YOU TAKE THE ROLE.
bookTeaser.quote: A rare book that captures what leadership actually feels like from the inside.

footer.brand: Strategy. Leadership. Performance. Impact. Short, focused leadership pieces for managers in motion. Created and produced by Bernhard Grohs. Narrated by Björn Grosso.

about.heroSub: A leadership podcast built around short, focused knowledge pieces on the challenges modern leaders face every day, created by Bernhard Grohs and narrated by Björn Grosso.
about.heading: WHY THIS PODCAST EXISTS.
about.pullQuote: Built for managers in motion, who don't have time for buzzwords but still want to think hard about the work.

p: Most management content is either too abstract to apply on a Tuesday morning or too tactical to teach you anything new. The Manager's Matrix sits in the middle: short, focused pieces, usually fifteen to twenty-five minutes, that take one real situation a manager faces and walk it down to something you can actually do.

The show organizes everything around four pillars: strategy, leadership, performance, and impact. Every episode lives at the intersection of at least two of them, which is also where most real management decisions actually happen.

Bernhard Grohs is a leadership writer based in Vienna. He's been a manager for fifteen years across early-stage startups, consultancies, and product teams. The podcast is the writing he doesn't have time to publish as essays, turned into a format you can listen to on the way to your next 1:1.

The episodes draw on his own day-to-day mistakes, public research from people like the HBR contributors he cites in the show notes, and the kind of frank conversations that usually only happen between trusted peers.

Each episode is narrated by Björn Grosso, an AI voice cloned and tuned to deliver the material with the warm, cinematic storytelling the format calls for. Björn is not a real person; he's a production choice. The decision was deliberate: an AI narrator lets the show ship two episodes a week without sacrificing the careful writing that goes into each one.
The ideas, the writing, and the editorial judgement are all human. Björn is the read.

Each episode begins as a written script, usually responding to a piece of research, a leadership article, or a moment from the host's week. The draft gets a structural pass for pacing and a line edit for voice, then runs through the production pipeline that generates Björn's read, the cold-open, and the chapter markers.
Episode ideas, corrections, gentle disagreements, and warm hellos all welcome at bernhard@grohs.org. For press and speaking, same address; please put “press” in the subject so it doesn't get lost.

book.subtitle: Leadership in the Age of Change
book.quote: A rare book that captures what leadership actually feels like from the inside.
book.bodyHeading: WHAT HAPPENS WHEN THE BIGGEST TRANSFORMATION ISN'T DIGITAL, BUT PERSONAL?
book.lead: The Manager's Matrix follows an IT leader navigating the gap between how organizations talk about leadership and how it actually feels day to day.
paras: Through the lens of business fiction, it explores the tensions every leader carries: between confidence and doubt, passion and restraint, vision and reality.

We meet Björn Grosso, a capable, respected leader standing at a professional crossroads. After years of excelling as the reliable expert and fixer, he joins a new company with a different culture, different expectations, and a team he didn't choose and didn't build. From day one he steps into a room where loyalties have already formed and every eye is silently asking: “Who are you, and why should we follow you?”

The challenges he faces aren't about tasks anymore; they're about transformation. The very things that once made him successful (quick problem-solving, hands-on execution, relentless availability) have quietly become liabilities. It's not a ladder he's climbing. It's a matrix.
This is not a management textbook. This is the story no one tells you before you take the role.
book.ctaText: First edition. Available in print. Reach out for ordering details, review copies, or bulk requests for your leadership team.
book.dedication: For Isabella, who reminds me that leadership begins at home.
